code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test03-NOV-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction and/or underlying metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or altered red cell turnover. Recommend further nephrological and metabolic evaluation, and correlation with clinical findings.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
